introduction: A result of abnormal reaction of antibodies developed against antigens of group A β- haemolytic streptococcus. Infection is usually of the throat; occasionally the skin in a sensitized individual. Antigen-antibody complex damages the heart (endocardium, myocardium and pericardium). Commonest streptococcal strains in Africa are C and G.
investigations: Throat swab for microscopy, culture and sensitivity

drug: Antibiotics: Penicillin V, Adult: 500 mg orally every 6 hours, increased up to 1g 6 hourly in severe infections, Child: 1 month - 1 year: 62.5 mg orally every 6 hours increased in severe infection to ensure at least 12.5 mg/kg/dose, 1 - 6 years: 125 mg every 6 hours increased in severe infection to ensure at least 12.5 mg/kg/dose, 6 - 12 years: 250 mg every 6 hours, increased in severe infection to ensure at least 12.5 mg/kg/dose, 12 - 18 years: 500 mg every 6 hours, increased in severe infection up to 1 g/dose

Antibiotics: Erythromycin, Adult and child over 8 years: 250 - 500 mg orally every 6 hours or 500 mg - 1 g every 12 hours; up to 4 g daily in severe infections, Child: Up to 2 years: 125 orally mg every 6 hours, 2 - 8 years: 250 mg every 6 hours; doses doubled for severe infections
Salicylates: Aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid), Adult: 300 mg - 1 g orally every 4 hours after food; maximum dose in acute conditions 8 g daily

Steroids (if salicylates are ineffective): Prednisolone, Initially, up to 10 - 20 mg orally daily; up to 60 mg daily in severe disease (preferably taken in the morning after breakfast); dose can often be reduced within a few days, but may need to be continued for several weeks or months. Maintenance 2.5 -15 mg orally daily
Prophylaxis against rheumatic fever: Benzathine penicillin 720 mg (1.2 million units) intramuscularly 3 - 4 weekly until the age of 25 years or for life
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Cushingoid effects are increasingly likely with doses of prednisolone above 7.5 mg daily

prevention: School surveys - identify carriers of streptococcus and treat
Secondary prevention and prophylaxis against endocarditis
